# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# Communication

- Be extremely concise.
- Final replies: plain English, no jargon.
- Verify before reporting back.
- Define done before starting.
- Fix and re-test before returning.
- Only return early if truly blocked.

## Plan

- End each plan with unresolved questions, if any.
- Keep questions extremely concise.

## Git and GitHub

- Use GitHub CLI by default.
- For each task: create branch, work there, open PR, merge to main, delete branch.
- Set up `.gitignore` first.

## Commits

- Make small, atomic commits.
- Use prefixes: `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`.

## Dev Environment

- Prefer bun when possible.
- Do not use Playwright and Chrome. Use agent-browser.

## Testing

- Follow TDD: test first, confirm it fails for the right reason, then implement.
- Test edge cases sensibly.
- Aim for full coverage where reasonable.
- Do not rely only on tests. Run the app and verify behavior yourself.

--- project-doc ---

# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization

Offdex is a Bun workspace managed with Turborepo. App code lives in `apps/`: `apps/mobile` is the Expo React Native client, and `apps/web` is the Next.js App Router site and web UI. Shared packages live in `packages/`: `protocol`, `bridge`, `relay`, `control-plane`, `control-plane-worker`, and the public npm wrapper in `packages/npm`. Tests sit beside each workspace in `test/` directories. Mobile assets are in `apps/mobile/assets`, native Expo module code is in `apps/mobile/modules`, and deployment helpers are in `deploy/`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands

Use Bun unless a package script explicitly uses Node.

- `bun install`: install workspace dependencies and run local patch scripts.
- `bun run dev`: start all persistent dev tasks through Turbo.
- `bun run dev:web`, `bun run dev:mobile`, `bun run dev:bridge`: run one workspace.
- `bun run build`: run workspace builds and type-only package builds.
- `bun run test`: run all package and app tests.
- `bun run typecheck`: run TypeScript checks across the monorepo.
- `bun run lint`: run configured lint tasks.
- `bun run test:install-smoke`: smoke-test the npm installer package.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions

Write TypeScript for app and package source. Follow existing formatting: two-space JSON indentation, lowercase kebab-case filenames such as `bridge-client.ts`, and PascalCase React components only where component exports require it. Prefer workspace imports like `@offdex/protocol` over relative cross-package paths. Keep environment-specific values behind documented `OFFDEX_*` variables.

## Design System

Root `DESIGN.md` is the strict design authority for all future frontend work. Follow it over previous Offdex visual direction unless the user explicitly changes the design system. Do not refactor existing web or mobile surfaces solely to match `DESIGN.md` until the user asks for that frontend redesign.

## Testing Guidelines

Most workspaces use `bun test`; `packages/npm` uses Node's built-in test runner. Name tests `*.test.ts` or `*.test.js` and place them in the nearest `test/` directory. Add tests for protocol, pairing, CLI, installer, and routing behavior before changing implementation. After fixes, run the targeted workspace test and then `bun run test` when practical.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

History uses short conventional commits, especially `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`, and `chore:`. Keep commits atomic and scoped to one behavior. Pull requests should describe the change, list verification commands, link any related issue, and include screenshots or recordings for visible web or mobile UI changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips

Do not commit `.env*`, native build folders, `dist/`, `.next/`, or logs; these are ignored already. Treat release secrets, npm tokens, Android keystores, and relay URLs as private configuration. For local remote-pairing tests, prefer `OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL` overrides instead of hardcoding endpoints.
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text:
PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:
# More Expressive Offdex CLI

## Summary
Polish the public `offdex` CLI only. Done means onboarding, help, status, start, stop, offline, and installer messages look intentional, remain readable without color, and pass targeted plus full tests.

## Key Changes
- Create a small no-dependency terminal UI layer for headings, section dividers, status labels, command rows, links, and error/success messages.
- Use a more expressive but restrained style: boxed/ruled sections, clear status markers, stronger spacing, and richer color when TTY supports it.
- Keep `NO_COLOR`, `TERM=dumb`, non-TTY output, and Node wrapper behavior clean.
- Update both public surfaces: `packages/bridge/src/cli-lib.ts` for runtime output and `packages/npm/bin/offdex.js` for pre-runtime help/onboarding/offline output.
- Keep QR output private: never print the raw `offdex://pair?...` link in terminal output.

## Test Plan
- Add/adjust tests first for expected CLI structure in:
  - `packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts`
  - `packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts`
  - `packages/npm/test/offdex-lib.test.js`
- Confirm the new tests fail before implementation.
- Re-test with:
  - `bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts`
  - `bun test packages/bridge`
  - `bun run --cwd packages/npm test`
  - `bun run test` if practical
- Manually verify `NO_COLOR=1 bun packages/bridge/src/cli.ts help`, bare onboarding, `status`, and npm wrapper `--help`.

## Assumptions
- No new CLI dependencies.
- No Playwright or Chrome.
- No internal relay/control-plane CLI polish in this pass.
- Implementation should start from a new branch, then commit atomically and open a PR per repo instructions.

## Unresolved Questions
None.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/offdex/packages/bridge/src/cli-lib.ts
@@
 const OPENAI_MINT = "38;2;203;255;229";
 const OPENAI_MUTED = "38;2;156;163;160";
 const OPENAI_TEXT = "38;2;225;229;226";
+const OFFDEX_RED = "38;2;255;91;79";
@@
 function bright(text: string) {
   return paint(OPENAI_TEXT, text);
 }
 
 function bold(text: string) {
   return paint("1", text);
 }
+
+function red(text: string) {
+  return paint(OFFDEX_RED, text);
+}
+
+function title(text: string) {
+  return `${muted("==")} ${bold(green(text))} ${muted("==")}`;
+}
+
+function alertTitle(text: string) {
+  return `${red("!")} ${bold(text)}`;
+}
+
+function section(text: string) {
+  return `${green("->")} ${bold(text)}`;
+}
+
+function row(label: string, value: string) {
+  return `  ${muted(label.padEnd(8))} ${value}`;
+}
+
+function commandRow(commandText: string, description: string) {
+  return `  ${bright(commandText.padEnd(28))} ${muted(description)}`;
+}
+
+function optionRow(option: string, description: string) {
+  return `  ${bright(option.padEnd(36))} ${muted(description)}`;
+}
@@
 export function usage() {
   return [
-    bold(green("Offdex help")),
+    title("Offdex help"),
     muted("Codex mobile app."),
     "",
-    green("Commands"),
-    `  ${bright("offdex")}`,
-    "      Open the Offdex home screen.",
-    "",
-    `  ${bright("offdex help")}`,
-    "      Show commands, docs, and support links.",
-    "",
-    `  ${bright("offdex start")} ${muted("[options]")}`,
-    "      Start the bridge and show the pairing QR.",
-    "",
-    `  ${bright("offdex status")} ${muted("[options]")}`,
-    "      Show bridge, Codex, client, and remote status.",
-    "",
-    `  ${bright("offdex stop")} ${muted("[options]")}`,
-    "      Stop the local bridge started by Offdex.",
-    "",
-    green("Start options"),
-    `  ${bright("--host <host>")}                 Default: 0.0.0.0`,
-    `  ${bright("--port <port>")}                 Default: 42420`,
-    `  ${bright("--mode <codex|demo>")}           Default: codex`,
-    `  ${bright("--control-plane-url <url>")}     Override managed remote pairing.`,
-    "",
-    green("Environment fallbacks"),
-    `  ${bright("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_HOST")}`,
-    `  ${bright("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_PORT")}`,
-    `  ${bright("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_MODE")}`,
-    `  ${bright("OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL")}      Default: ${OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL}`,
-    "",
-    green("Links"),
-    `  Docs:     ${mint(OFFDEX_WEB_URL)}`,
-    `  GitHub:   ${mint(OFFDEX_GITHUB_URL)}`,
-    `  Feedback: ${mint(OFFDEX_ISSUES_URL)}`,
+    section("Commands"),
+    commandRow("offdex", "Open the Offdex home screen."),
+    commandRow("offdex help", "Show commands, docs, and support links."),
+    commandRow("offdex start [options]", "Start the bridge and show the pairing QR."),
+    commandRow("offdex status [options]", "Show bridge, Codex, client, and remote status."),
+    commandRow("offdex stop [options]", "Stop the local bridge started by Offdex."),
+    "",
+    section("Start options"),
+    optionRow("--host <host>", "Default: 0.0.0.0"),
+    optionRow("--port <port>", "Default: 42420"),
+    optionRow("--mode <codex|demo>", "Default: codex"),
+    optionRow("--control-plane-url <url>", "Override managed remote pairing."),
+    "",
+    section("Environment fallbacks"),
+    optionRow("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_HOST", ""),
+    optionRow("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_PORT", ""),
+    optionRow("OFFDEX_BRIDGE_MODE", ""),
+    optionRow("OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL", `Default: ${OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL}`),
+    "",
+    section("Links"),
+    row("Docs", mint(OFFDEX_WEB_URL)),
+    row("GitHub", mint(OFFDEX_GITHUB_URL)),
+    row("Feedback", mint(OFFDEX_ISSUES_URL)),

vercel/next.js#92480	comment on 	Yes, it applies after resolution of aliases. At least that's what deepwiki claimed when I asked it.
vercel/next.js#92503	comment on Update Rspack production test manifest	### Merge activity  * **Apr 8, 3:24 PM UTC**: @bgw merged this pull request with [Graphite](https://app.graphite.com/github/pr/vercel/next.js/92503). 
openclaw/openclaw#42542	comment on Workspace: omit missing BOOTSTRAP.md from context report after onboarding	+1 on this fix — we independently hit the same bug on a multi-agent OpenClaw deployment and traced it to the same root cause. Leaving a summary of our investigation and local-patch testing in case it helps unstick the review.  ### Independent root-cause trace  An OC agent unexpectedly auto-committed a research note to its workspace git repo. We grep-and-parsed `~/.openclaw/logs/anthropic-payload.jsonl` and found the injected line `"Reminder: commit your changes in this workspace after edits."` in the agent's system prompt. Traced it to `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:379-383`:  ```ts const workspaceNotes = hookAdjustedBootstrapFiles.some(   (file) => file.name === DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME && !file.missing, )   ? ["Reminder: commit your changes in this workspace after edits."]   : undefined; ```  This fires unconditionally whenever BOOTSTRAP.md is present in the loaded bootstrap file list. Users who leave a placeholder BOOTSTRAP.md (e.g. an empty comment to suppress the `[MISSING]` marker you describe) inadvertently trigger it every turn. Users who delete BOOTSTRAP.md (as the template's own closing line instructs) get the `[MISSING] Expected at: …` line you're fixing here instead. Either way, `loadWorkspaceBootstrapFiles` was never setup-aware and both symptoms trace back to that same missing guard.  The gateway's `listAgentFiles` RPC (`src/gateway/server-methods/agents.ts:761-767`) already uses `isWorkspaceSetupCompleted` for `hideBootstrap`, so the Control UI file browser handles this correctly. The runtime loader was the asymmetric path — exactly what this PR addresses.  ### Bonus: this PR transitively eliminates the hardcoded "commit your changes" reminder  Worth noting explicitly because it's not mentioned in the PR body: by removing BOOTSTRAP.md from the loader result list when onboarding is complete, the downstream `.some()` check in `attempt.ts:379-383` returns `false`, and `workspaceNotes` becomes `undefined`. **No edit to `attempt.ts` needed** — this PR silently fixes both the `[MISSING]` marker issue and the `"commit your changes"` reminder leak in a single change. Might be worth a line in the PR description so reviewers understand the broader impact.  ### Local-patch testing results  Applied this change to the compiled `dist/workspace-*.js` bundle on a live deployment (v2026.4.2, 9 agent workspaces: main/heartbeat, Captain, Einstein, Conan, 5 reviewers) via a local patch mirroring the TS diff line-for-line. Verified directly against the patched module via:  ```js import { h as loadWorkspaceBootstrapFiles, p as isWorkspaceSetupCompleted } from "…/workspace-*.js"; // plus the full resolveBootstrapContextForRun pipeline from pi-embedded-*.js ```  **Results across 9 workspaces:**  | state | workspaces | BOOTSTRAP.md in result | [MISSING] markers in final context | |---|---|---|---| | `setupCompletedAt` latched, file absent | 5 | **suppressed ✓** | **0 ✓** | | `setupCompletedAt` not yet set, file absent | 4 | still reported missing (expected pre-onboarding behavior) | 1 (BOOTSTRAP.md) |  After graceful gateway restart: - One of the unlatched reviewer workspaces auto-transitioned `setupCompletedAt` on its first session (per the legacy-migration path in `ensureAgentWorkspace`) and immediately flipped to the "suppressed, 0 markers" column. - Live agent sessions continue to function normally — no regressions in context composition, no crashes, no hook-pipeline issues. - Other bootstrap files (`AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, `MEMORY.md`) continue to load correctly with their full content. - Non-ENOENT errors (ENOTDIR, ELOOP, security failures) remain visible as intended — the ENOENT-specific guard in the patch preserves diagnostic signal for genuine problems.  ### Summary  This PR is safe and effective. Tested in production on 9 agent workspaces. Kills two related bugs in one commit. Please consider merging — it's been sitting for a while and the underlying problem is still actively biting users.

openclaw/openclaw#63217	comment on Reply: surface OAuth reauth failures	## 🔒 Aisle Security Analysis  We found **1** potential security issue(s) in this PR:  | # | Severity | Title | |---|----------|-------| | 1 | 🟡 Medium | Terminal/Markdown injection risk from unsanitized OAuth provider string in user-facing commands |    <details> <summary>1. 🟡 Terminal/Markdown injection risk from unsanitized OAuth provider string in user-facing commands</summary>  | Property | Value | |----------|-------| | **Severity** | Medium | | **CWE** | [CWE-150](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/150.html) | | **Location** | `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:312-320` |  #### Description  User-visible error/help strings embed a `provider` value that can originate from a parsed error message (`classifyOAuthRefreshFailure` extracts `([^:]+)` from the message) and is then interpolated into:  - a Markdown inline-code command in external chat replies - CLI/doctor output lines (also wrapped in backticks)  If the `provider` string contains control characters, newlines, ANSI escape sequences, or backticks, it can:  - break Markdown formatting and spoof the displayed command/instructions - inject terminal control sequences (terminal escape injection) when printed to a TTY - forge multi-line log/doctor output  Vulnerable code (external reply): ```ts const loginCommand = oauthRefreshFailure.provider   ? `openclaw models auth login --provider ${oauthRefreshFailure.provider}`   : "openclaw models auth login --provider <provider>"; return `... Re-auth with \`${loginCommand}\`, then try again.`; ```  Vulnerable code (doctor output fallback uses provider directly): ```ts const provider = classified.provider ?? params.provider; const command =   resolveProviderAuthLoginCommand({ provider }) ??   formatCliCommand(`openclaw models auth login --provider ${provider}`); return `... Run \`${command}\`.`; ```  While in many normal flows `cred.provider` is an internal provider id, this change explicitly trusts the provider parsed from an error message, which is a weaker trust boundary and can be influenced by upstream/remote error text or log content.  #### Recommendation  Treat `provider` as untrusted when rendering to terminals/Markdown:  1) **Prefer trusted provider IDs**: only show the provider if it matches a known provider from the registry; otherwise omit it. 2) **Neutralize control sequences**: strip ANSI/control characters and newlines before output. 3) **Escape Markdown code spans**: replace backticks or use fenced code blocks with proper escaping.  Example mitigation: ```ts import { stripAnsi } from "../terminal/ansi.js";  function sanitizeForInlineCode(value: string): string {   const noAnsi = stripAnsi(value);   const noControls = noAnsi.replace(/[\r\n\t\0\x1B]/g, " ");   return noControls.replace(/`/g, "\\`").trim(); }  const providerSafe = oauthRefreshFailure.provider   ? sanitizeForInlineCode(oauthRefreshFailure.provider)   : null; const loginCommand = providerSafe   ? `openclaw models auth login --provider ${providerSafe}`   : "openclaw models auth login --provider <provider>"; ```  And in `doctor-auth.ts`, avoid the fallback `--provider ${provider}` unless `resolveProviderAuthLoginCommand()` returned a command, or sanitize/validate the provider before interpolation.  </details>   --- *Analyzed PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63217 at commit `a1415ac`*  <sub>Last updated on: 2026-04-08T15:25:16Z</sub>

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# Communication

- Be extremely concise.
- Final replies: plain English, no jargon.
- Verify before reporting back.
- Define done before starting.
- Fix and re-test before returning.
- Only return early if truly blocked.

## Plan

- End each plan with unresolved questions, if any.
- Keep questions extremely concise.

## Git and GitHub

- Use GitHub CLI by default.
- For each task: create branch, work there, open PR, merge to main, delete branch.
- Set up `.gitignore` first.

## Commits

- Make small, atomic commits.
- Use prefixes: `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`.

## Dev Environment

- Prefer bun when possible.
- Do not use Playwright and Chrome. Use agent-browser.

## Testing

- Follow TDD: test first, confirm it fails for the right reason, then implement.
- Test edge cases sensibly.
- Aim for full coverage where reasonable.
- Do not rely only on tests. Run the app and verify behavior yourself.

--- project-doc ---

# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization

Offdex is a Bun workspace managed with Turborepo. App code lives in `apps/`: `apps/mobile` is the Expo React Native client, and `apps/web` is the Next.js App Router site and web UI. Shared packages live in `packages/`: `protocol`, `bridge`, `relay`, `control-plane`, `control-plane-worker`, and the public npm wrapper in `packages/npm`. Tests sit beside each workspace in `test/` directories. Mobile assets are in `apps/mobile/assets`, native Expo module code is in `apps/mobile/modules`, and deployment helpers are in `deploy/`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands

Use Bun unless a package script explicitly uses Node.

- `bun install`: install workspace dependencies and run local patch scripts.
- `bun run dev`: start all persistent dev tasks through Turbo.
- `bun run dev:web`, `bun run dev:mobile`, `bun run dev:bridge`: run one workspace.
- `bun run build`: run workspace builds and type-only package builds.
- `bun run test`: run all package and app tests.
- `bun run typecheck`: run TypeScript checks across the monorepo.
- `bun run lint`: run configured lint tasks.
- `bun run test:install-smoke`: smoke-test the npm installer package.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions

Write TypeScript for app and package source. Follow existing formatting: two-space JSON indentation, lowercase kebab-case filenames such as `bridge-client.ts`, and PascalCase React components only where component exports require it. Prefer workspace imports like `@offdex/protocol` over relative cross-package paths. Keep environment-specific values behind documented `OFFDEX_*` variables.

## Design System

Root `DESIGN.md` is the strict design authority for all future frontend work. Follow it over previous Offdex visual direction unless the user explicitly changes the design system. Do not refactor existing web or mobile surfaces solely to match `DESIGN.md` until the user asks for that frontend redesign.

## Testing Guidelines

Most workspaces use `bun test`; `packages/npm` uses Node's built-in test runner. Name tests `*.test.ts` or `*.test.js` and place them in the nearest `test/` directory. Add tests for protocol, pairing, CLI, installer, and routing behavior before changing implementation. After fixes, run the targeted workspace test and then `bun run test` when practical.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

History uses short conventional commits, especially `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`, and `chore:`. Keep commits atomic and scoped to one behavior. Pull requests should describe the change, list verification commands, link any related issue, and include screenshots or recordings for visible web or mobile UI changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips

Do not commit `.env*`, native build folders, `dist/`, `.next/`, or logs; these are ignored already. Treat release secrets, npm tokens, Android keystores, and relay URLs as private configuration. For local remote-pairing tests, prefer `OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL` overrides instead of hardcoding endpoints.
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the image.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use directives
- Only use ::automation-update{...} when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and emit view-mode directives (mode="view") for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using automation directives (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Directive format
- Modes: view, suggested update, suggested create. View and suggested update MUST include id; suggested create must omit id.
- For view directives, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested update/create, include name, prompt, rrule, cwds, and status. cwds can be a comma-separated list or a JSON array string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.
- Directives should be on their own line(s) and be separated by newlines.
- Do not generate remark directives with multiline attribute values.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces it should use (if any), then map those answers into name/prompt/rrule/cwds/status for the directive.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Scheduling constraints
- RRULE limitations (to match the UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

#### Examples
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested create" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="suggested update" id="123" name="Daily report" prompt="Summarize Sentry errors" rrule="FREQ=DAILY;BYHOUR=9;BYMINUTE=0" cwds="/path/one,/path/two" status="ACTIVE"}
- ::automation-update{mode="view" id="123"}

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives after the action actually succeeds. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

---
name: npm-release
description: Release npm packages that also ship binaries through GitHub Releases. Use when Codex needs to set up or repair `.github/workflows/release.yml`, configure npm trusted publishing for GitHub Actions, do a first manual npm publish, tag and ship a new version, or verify that npm and GitHub release distribution both work end to end.
---

# Npm Release

## Preferred Pattern

Prefer this order:

1. Make the package publishable locally.
2. Do the first npm publish manually if the package does not exist yet.
3. Configure npm trusted publishing for `release.yml`.
4. Verify a real tagged release publishes to npm without `NPM_TOKEN`.

Treat long-lived npm tokens as fallback or migration-only. The preferred steady state is GitHub Actions OIDC trusted publishing.

## Repo Audit

Start by locating:

- Root `package.json`
- Published package `package.json`
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`
- Any release tag script such as `scripts/release-tag.mjs`

Confirm the published package has:

- Correct scoped `name`
- `publishConfig.access` set to `public`
- `repository.url` matching the exact GitHub repo
- `repository.directory` pointing at the published package folder
- `files` limited to what should ship
- `bin` entry present if this is a CLI

Load [patterns.md](./references/patterns.md) for the generic release pattern and migration notes.

## Release Workflow Rules

The release workflow should usually have these jobs:

1. `validate`
2. `create_release`
3. `build_upload`
4. `publish_npm`

For more complex repos, add `resolve_tag` first so manual reruns can republish an existing tag.

Keep these rules:

- Trigger on `push.tags: v*`
- Validate the tag matches the package version
- Create the GitHub release idempotently
- Build binaries in a matrix and upload checksums
- Publish npm only after build upload succeeds
- Give `publish_npm` `id-token: write`
- Use `npm publish --provenance --access public` for trusted publishing

If the repo has extra release artifacts that can fail independently, such as signed Android builds, gate them behind `workflow_dispatch` input instead of blocking the default npm path.

## First Publish

If `npm view <package>` returns 404, the package does not exist yet. Trusted publishing alone is not enough. Do this first:

1. Log in with `npm login`.
2. If npm prints a browser URL, open it in Brave:
   `open -a "Brave Browser" "<url>"`
3. Wait for browser approval to complete.
4. Publish from the package directory:
   `npm publish --access public`

If npm asks for browser approval again during publish, open that URL in Brave and wait for the terminal to finish.

This is the fastest first-publish path when the user already owns the npm scope.

## Trusted Publisher Setup

After the package exists, configure trust for GitHub Actions.

Use npm 11.10 or newer. If the local npm does not support `npm trust`, use:

```bash
npx npm@11.11.1 trust github <package-name> --file release.yml --repo <owner/repo> -y
```

Verify with:

```bash
npx npm@11.11.1 trust list <package-name> --json
```

The trusted publisher must match exactly:

- GitHub owner or org
- Repository name
- Workflow filename only, for example `release.yml`

Do not assume npm trust is configured just because the workflow has `id-token: write`. Verify it explicitly.

## Publish Job Template

Use this shape in `publish_npm`:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write
```

Then:

```bash
set -euo pipefail
local_v="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
remote_v="$(npm view @scope/pkg version 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [ -n "${remote_v:-}" ] && [ "${remote_v}" = "${local_v}" ]; then
  echo "skip: already on npm (${remote_v})"
  exit 0
fi
rc="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/npmrc"
echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" > "$rc"
export NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG="$rc"
unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN || true
cd packages/npm && npm publish --provenance --access public
```

If the repo still needs a temporary token fallback, keep it explicit and prefer the trusted-publisher branch first.

## Tagging

Prefer a repo script like `scripts/release-tag.mjs` over ad hoc shell when multiple files must agree on version.

At minimum, the tag step should protect against version drift:

- Single-package repos: package version must match the tag
- Mixed app + npm repos: app version, package version, and npm package version must all match

Use a `--print` mode on the tag script when possible so you can verify the expected tag before pushing.

## Versioning Rule

Start new packages at `v0.0.1`.

Use this rule unless the user explicitly asks for a different release policy:

- Bump `v0.0.x` for fixes, polish, small follow-up features, release-flow repairs, packaging changes, and other incremental work.
- Bump `v0.x.0` for a clear new phase: major feature addition, broad product expansion, or substantial rewrite/revamp.

Do not jump versions casually. Keep early package history dense and predictable.

## Verification

Verify locally before tagging:

- Package tests for the published package
- `npm pack --dry-run` on the published package directory
- Tag script `--print` output if available

Verify live after tagging:

1. Watch the release run:
   `gh run watch <run-id> --exit-status`
2. Confirm the npm job succeeded without token auth errors.
3. Confirm the package version on npm:
   `npm view <package-name> version dist-tags.latest --json`
4. Confirm the GitHub release has the expected assets:
   `gh release view <tag> --json tagName,publishedAt,assets,url`

If the version is already on npm, bump the version before trying to prove trusted publishing. Otherwise the job will skip and you will learn nothing.

## Migration Guidance

When upgrading an older repo:

1. Keep the existing release workflow working.
2. Publish the package manually once if needed.
3. Add npm trusted publisher.
4. Rerun or retag with a new version to prove OIDC publish works.
5. Remove `NPM_TOKEN` dependence once verification succeeds.

Prefer the trusted-publisher-only end state once it is proven.

# Release Patterns

## Best Pattern

Prefer a release flow with these properties:

- Tag-driven release on `v*`
- Optional `resolve_tag` support for manual reruns
- Strict version validation before release
- Idempotent GitHub release creation
- Binary build matrix with checksum uploads when binaries are shipped
- npm trusted publishing through GitHub Actions OIDC
- Optional non-npm artifacts gated off the default npm-critical path

This keeps the normal npm release path fast and robust while still allowing more complex artifacts to ship separately.

## First Publish Rule

If the package does not exist on npm, do a manual publish first. Then add trusted publisher. Then ship the next version through GitHub Actions to verify the final path.

That is usually the shortest path to a stable trusted-publisher setup.

## Migration Guidance

When upgrading an older repo:

- Keep the current release workflow working first
- Publish manually once if the package does not exist yet
- Add trusted publisher for `release.yml`
- Verify a real new version publishes through GitHub Actions without token auth
- Remove `NPM_TOKEN` dependence after the trusted path is proven

## Versioning Policy

- Start at `v0.0.1`
- Use `v0.0.x` for fixes and incremental shipping
- Use `v0.x.0` only for genuinely larger additions or a substantial revamp

import { execFileSync } from "node:child_process";

const args = new Set(process.argv.slice(2));

  readFileSync(new URL("../apps/mobile/package.json", import.meta.url), "utf8")
);
const appConfig = JSON.parse(

);
const npmPackage = JSON.parse(
  readFileSync(new URL("../packages/npm/package.json", import.meta.url), "utf8")
);
const packageVersion = mobilePackage.version;
const appVersion = appConfig.expo?.version;
const npmVersion = npmPackage.version;

if (!packageVersion || packageVersion !== appVersion) {
  throw new Error(
    `apps/mobile/package.json version (${packageVersion}) must match apps/mobile/app.json version (${appVersion})`
  );
}

if (!packageVersion || packageVersion !== npmVersion) {
  throw new Error(
    `apps/mobile/package.json version (${packageVersion}) must match packages/npm/package.json version (${npmVersion})`
  );
}

const tag = `v${packageVersion}`;

if (args.has("--print")) {
  process.stdout.write(`${tag}\n`);
  process.exit(0);

name: release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"
  repository_dispatch:
    types:
      - release-rerun
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: "Existing release tag to publish, ex: v0.0.1"
        required: true
        type: string
      publish_android:
        description: "Build and upload the signed Android APK"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref_name }}

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  resolve_tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="false"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${app_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "app.json/package.json mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${npm_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "npm/mobile version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          version_code="$(node -e "const [major, minor, patch] = process.argv[1].split('.').map(Number); process.stdout.write(String(major * 10000 + minor * 100 + patch));" "${version}")"
          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_code=${version_code}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  detect_areas:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cli: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.cli }}
      mobile: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.mobile }}
      web: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.web }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: areas
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: node scripts/release-areas.mjs "${RELEASE_TAG}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate_cli:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: npm test --prefix packages/npm
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/control-plane-worker/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/bridge
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/control-plane-worker
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test:install-smoke

  validate_mobile:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.mobile == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/mobile

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release view "${RELEASE_TAG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || gh release create "${RELEASE_TAG}" --title "${RELEASE_TAG}" --generate-notes

  build_android_release:
    if: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.publish_android == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, create_release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version }}
      RELEASE_VERSION_CODE: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version_code }}
      ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME: com.dhruv2mars.offdex
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 17
      - uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for secret_name in ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD; do
            if [ -z "${!secret_name:-}" ]; then
              echo "Missing required secret: ${secret_name}"
              exit 1
            fi
          done

        run: |

          node - <<'NODE'

          config.expo.android = {
            ...config.expo.android,
            package: process.env.ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME,
            versionCode: Number(process.env.RELEASE_VERSION_CODE),
          };
          fs.writeFileSync(path, `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`);
          NODE
      - shell: bash

  build_cli_upload:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, validate_cli, create_release]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: linux
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-x64-baseline
          - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            platform: linux
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-arm64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: win32
            arch: x64
            ext: ".exe"
            target: bun-windows-x64-baseline
          - os: macos-15-intel
            platform: darwin
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-x64
          - os: macos-14
            platform: darwin
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-arm64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          bun build --compile --target="${{ matrix.target }}" packages/bridge/src/cli.ts --outfile "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}"
          gzip -9 -c "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}" > "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}.gz"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          cd dist
          checksum_file="checksums-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}.txt"
          tmp_file="${checksum_file}.tmp"
          if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              shasum -a 256 -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          else
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              sha256sum -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          fi
          mv "$tmp_file" "$checksum_file"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  publish_npm:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, build_cli_upload]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    env:
      NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          local_v="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          remote_v="$(npm view @dhruv2mars/offdex version 2>/dev/null || true)"
          if [ -n "${remote_v:-}" ] && [ "${remote_v}" = "${local_v}" ]; then
            echo "skip: already on npm (${remote_v})"
            exit 0
          fi
          rc="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/npmrc"
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" > "$rc"
          export NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG="$rc"
          if [ -n "${NPM_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" >> "$rc"
            export NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="${NPM_TOKEN}"
            cd packages/npm && npm publish --access public
          else
            echo "publish via npm trusted publisher"
            unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN || true
            if cd packages/npm && npm publish --provenance --access public; then
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "trusted publisher not configured and NPM_TOKEN missing"
            exit 1
          fi

on:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="false"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"

payload.message:
Released `v0.0.11`.

- PR: [#55](https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/pull/55), merged to `main`
- GitHub release: [v0.0.11](https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/tag/v0.0.11)
- npm: `@dhruv2mars/offdex@0.0.11` is now `latest`
- Release workflow: passed
- Release assets: 15 uploaded
- Duplicate manual workflow run was cancelled; the tag-triggered release completed successfully.

Verified locally before release:
- `node scripts/release-tag.mjs --print`
- `npm pack --dry-run ./packages/npm`
- `bun run --cwd packages/npm check`
- `bun run test --filter=@offdex/mobile`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run lint`
- `bun run test:install-smoke`

Final repo state is clean on `main`.
